messages.content: You are the planning module of a patent-drafting agent. Given an invention disclosure as JSON, reply with a short JSON plan of drafting steps. Reply with JSON only.

{"id":"uspto-18797574","title":"SELF-ATTENTION IN HOMOMORPHIC ENCRYPTION DEEP LEARNING ARCHITECTURES","inventors":["Allon Adir","Ramy Masalha","Reut Moshe","OMRI SOCEANU","Itamar Zimerman"],"technology_class":"H04L","summary":"Mechanisms are provided for optimizing a deep learning (DL) computer model for homomorphic encryption (HE) workload processing. The mechanisms receive an original DL computer model architecture that is to be optimized for HE workload processing, and modifying the original DL computer model architecture by replacing a self-attention layer of the original DL computer model with an HE friendly self-attention layer that comprises a Power SoftMax function that does not have exponent terms, to thereby generate a modified DL computer model architecture. The mechanisms execute a machine learning training of the modified DL computer model architecture, approximate one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate a trained HE optimized DL computer model, and output the trained HE optimized DL computer model for execution on HE workloads.","details":"This listing of claims will replace all prior versions and listings of claims in the subject application.
Listing of Claims:
1
1. (Currently Amended) A computer-implemented method for optimizing a deep learning (DL) computer model for homomorphic encryption (HE) workload processing, the computer- implemented method comprising:receiving an original DL computer model architecture that is to be optimized for HE workload processing;
modifying the original DL computer model architecture by replacing a self-attention layer of the original DL computer model with a HE friendly self-attention layer that comprises a Power SoftMax function that does not have exponent terms, to thereby generate a modified DL computer model architecture;
executing a machine learning training of the modified DL computer model architecture;
approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate a trained HE optimized DL computer model; and
outputting the trained HE optimized DL computer model for execution on HE workloads;

wherein the Power SoftMax function comprises polynomial terms to replace non- polynomial terms, the non-polynomial terms being exponent terms, the polynomial terms being power terms;
 and
wherein the Power SoftMax function employs an approximation of division terms over a scale-agnostic range.

Currently amended

2
2. (Currently Amended) The computer-implemented method of claim 1, 
wherein the Power SoftMax function replaces the
non-polynomial terms, in a SoftMax function of a
self-
attention layer of the original DL computer model architecture, with the
polynomial terms.

Currently amended

3
3. (Currently Amended) The computer-implemented method of claim 2, 
wherein the non- polynomial terms are the
exponent terms in the SoftMax function, and wherein the polynomial terms are power terms.
Currently amended

4
4. (Cancelled)
Canceled

5
5. (Original) The computer-implemented method of claim 1, 
wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate the trained HE optimized DL computer model is performed after training the modified DL computer model architecture.
Original

6
6. (Original) The computer-implemented method of claim 5, 
wherein approximating one of more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials comprises approximating a division operation of the Power SoftMax function with a polynomial.
Original

7
7. (Original) The computer-implemented method of claim 5, 
wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function comprises implementing a Length-Agnostic Power SoftMax function based on a non-encrypted L sequence length of an input ciphertext.
Original

8
8. (Original) The computer-implemented method of claim 1, 
wherein the original DL computer model architecture is a Transformer DL computer model architecture.
Original

9
9. (Original) The computer-implemented method of claim 1, 
further comprising:deploying the trained HE optimized DL computer model on one or more server computing systems for runtime execution; and
executing the trained HE optimized DL computer model on HE workloads received from one or more client computing devices.

Original

10
10. (Currently Amended) The computer-implemented method of claim 1, 
wherein the data processing system is the trained HE optimized DL computer model is hosted on a server side data processing system and the method is executed in response to a request from a client

computing device, wherein the request specifies the original DL computer model architecture and one or more characteristics of the HE workload processing to be performed by the original DL computer model.
Currently amended

11
11. (Currently Amended) A computer program product comprising a computer readable storage medium having a computer readable program stored therein, wherein the computer readable program, when executed on a computing system, causes the computing system to optimize a deep learning (DL) computer model for homomorphic encryption (HE) workload processing, at least by:receiving an original DL computer model architecture that is to be optimized for HE workload processing;
modifying the original DL computer model architecture by replacing a self-attention layer of the original DL computer model with a HE friendly self-attention layer that comprises a Power SoftMax function that does not have exponent terms, to thereby generate a modified DL computer model architecture;
executing a machine learning training of the modified DL computer model architecture;
approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate a trained HE optimized DL computer model; and
outputting the trained HE optimized DL computer model for execution on HE workloads;

wherein the Power SoftMax function comprises polynomial terms to replace non- polynomial terms, the non-polynomial terms being exponent terms, the polynomial terms being power terms;
 and
wherein the Power SoftMax function employs an approximation of division terms over a scale-agnostic range.

Currently amended

12
12. (Currently Amended) The computer program product of claim 11, 
wherein the Power SoftMax function replaces the
non-polynomial terms, in a SoftMax function of a
self-
attention layer of the original DL computer model architecture, with the
polynomial terms.

Currently amended

13
13. (Currently Amended) The computer program product of claim 12, 
wherein the non- polynomial terms are the
exponent terms in the SoftMax function, and wherein the polynomial terms are power terms.
Currently amended

14
14. (Cancelled)
Canceled

15
15. (Original) The computer program product of claim 11, 
wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate the trained HE optimized DL computer model is performed after training the modified DL computer model architecture.
Original

16
16. (Original) The computer program product of claim 15, 
wherein approximating one of more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials comprises approximating a division operation of the Power SoftMax function with a polynomial.
Original

17
17. (Original) The computer program product of claim 15, 
wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function comprises implementing a Length-Agnostic Power SoftMax function based on a non-encrypted L sequence length of an input ciphertext.
Original

18
18. (Original) The computer program product of claim 11, 
wherein the original DL computer model architecture is a Transformer DL computer model architecture.
Original

19
19. (Original) The computer program product of claim 11, 
wherein the computer readable program further causes the computing system to:deploy the trained HE optimized DL computer model on one or more server computing systems for runtime execution; and
execute the trained HE optimized DL computer model on HE workloads received from one or more client computing devices.

Original

20
20. (Currently Amended) An apparatus comprising:at least one processor; and

at least one memory coupled to the at least one processor, wherein the at least one memory comprises instructions which, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the at least one processor to optimize a deep learning (DL) computer model for homomorphic encryption (HE) workload processing, at least by:
receiving an original DL computer model architecture that is to be optimized for HE workload processing;
modifying the original DL computer model architecture by replacing a self-attention layer of the original DL computer model with a HE friendly self-attention layer that comprises a Power SoftMax function that does not have exponent terms, to thereby generate a modified DL computer model architecture;
executing a machine learning training of the modified DL computer model architecture;
approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate a trained HE optimized DL computer model; and
outputting the trained HE optimized DL computer model for execution on HE workloads;

wherein the Power SoftMax function comprises polynomial terms to replace non- polynomial terms, the non-polynomial terms being exponent terms, the polynomial terms being power terms;
 and
wherein the Power SoftMax function employs an approximation of division terms over a scale-agnostic range.

Currently amended

transcript.reply:
{
  "drafting_steps": [
    "Extract core technical elements: self-attention layer replacement, Power SoftMax without exponents, polynomial approximation, HE-friendly optimization, training workflow.",
    "Identify key claim dependencies: independent claim 1 defines method; claims 2-3 refine Power SoftMax specifics; claim 8 specifies Transformer architecture; claim 9 adds deployment; claim 10 adds client-server interaction; claims 11-13 cover program product; claims 20 covers apparatus.",
    "Structure independent claim hierarchy: method claim → program product → apparatus, maintaining amendment status (currently amended vs. original).",
    "Map dependent claim relationships: claim 2 depends on 1, claim 3 on 2, claim 5 on 1, claim 6 on 5, claim 7 on 5, claim 8 on 1, claim 9 on 1, claim 10 on 1, claim 11 on 1, claim 12 on 11, claim 13 on 12, claim 15 on 11, claim 16 on 15, claim 17 on 15, claim 18 on 11, claim 19 on 11, claim 20 on 1.",
    "Flag amendments/cancellations: mark claims 4, 14, 16, 17, 18, 19 as cancelled/original/amended per document; retain current amendment status in plan.",
    "Prioritize claim scope: focus on novel Power SoftMax approximation method and HE optimization workflow as core inventive concept.",
    "Plan claim language precision: define "HE friendly self-attention layer", "Power SoftMax without exponent terms", "scale-agnostic range approximation", and "Length-Agnostic" as critical technical terms.",
    "Allocate drafting resources: allocate 40% to independent claim refinement, 30% to dependent claim mapping, 20% to amendment status tracking, 10% to cross-reference consistency.",
    "Verify legal compliance: ensure all amendments are properly marked and dependent claim chains remain intact per USPTO formatting rules."
  ]
}

messages.content:
You are a patent attorney. Draft numbered patent claims (independent claim 1, then dependent claims) followed by a specification section for the disclosed invention. Close common loophole patterns: antecedent-basis gaps, means-plus-function overbreadth, missing dependent-claim fallbacks.

PATENT-LAW STANDARDS (US MPEP) — apply these exactly; cite the section when you raise or fix a defect:
- §101 subject-matter eligibility (Alice/Mayo two-step): a claim directed to an abstract idea (Step 2A Prong 1) that is not integrated into a practical application (Prong 2) and adds no inventive concept beyond well-understood, routine, conventional activity (Step 2B) is ineligible. Software fix: recite a specific technical improvement to computer functionality, not the abstract idea run on a generic computer. [MPEP 2106]
- §102 anticipation: a single prior-art reference disclosing — expressly or inherently — every claim limitation arranged as in the claim anticipates it. Fix: add a limitation no single reference discloses. [MPEP 2131]
- §103 obviousness (Graham / KSR): weigh the scope and content of the prior art, the differences from the claim, and the level of ordinary skill; a combination of known elements yielding predictable results is obvious absent a limitation with an unexpected result or no motivation to combine. Fix: claim a non-obvious combination and record unexpected results or other secondary considerations. [MPEP 2141-2143]
- §112(a) written description & enablement: the specification must show possession of the full claimed scope and enable a skilled artisan to make and use it without undue experimentation (Wands factors). Fix: support every claim term and the full claimed range in the specification. [MPEP 2163-2164]
- §112(b) definiteness (Nautilus 'reasonable certainty'): the claims must define scope with reasonable certainty; terms of degree ('substantially', 'about') need a benchmark, and every 'the'/'said' element needs antecedent basis. Fix: add antecedent basis and a definite standard for any relative term. [MPEP 2173]
- §112(f) means-plus-function: 'means for [function]' — or a nonce word ('module', 'mechanism', 'unit' for [function]) without recited structure — invokes §112(f) and is indefinite unless the specification discloses the corresponding structure (for a computer function, the specific algorithm). Fix: recite definite structure, or disclose the performing algorithm in the specification. [MPEP 2181]

Loophole guardrails retrieved from memory for this technology class — close each one with explicit claim language; if none are listed, draft with standard care:

    "Structure independent claim hierarchy: method claim → program product → apparatus, maintaining amendment status (currently amended vs. original).",
    "Map dependent claim relationships: claim 2 depends on 1, claim 3 on 2, claim 5 on 1, claim 6 on 5, claim 7 on 5, claim 8 on 1, claim 9 on 1, claim 10 on 1, claim 11 on 1, claim 12 on 11, claim 13 on 12, claim 15 on 11, claim 16 on 15, claim 17 on 15, claim 18 on 11, claim 19 on 11, claim 20 on 1.",
    "Flag amendments/cancellations: mark claims 4, 14, 16, 17, 18, 19 as cancelled/original/amended per document; retain current amendment status in plan.",
    "Prioritize claim scope: focus on novel Power SoftMax approximation method and HE optimization workflow as core inventive concept.",
    "Plan claim language precision: define "HE friendly self-attention layer", "Power SoftMax without exponent terms", "scale-agnostic range approximation", and "Length-Agnostic" as critical technical terms.",
    "Allocate drafting resources: allocate 40% to independent claim refinement, 30% to dependent claim mapping, 20% to amendment status tracking, 10% to cross-reference consistency.",
    "Verify legal compliance: ensure all amendments are properly marked and dependent claim chains remain intact per USPTO formatting rules."
  ]
}

transcript.reply:
**1.**  
A computer-implemented method for optimizing a deep learning (DL) computer model for homomorphic encryption (HE) workload processing, the method comprising:  
receiving an original DL computer model architecture that is to be optimized for HE workload processing;  
modifying the original DL computer model architecture by replacing a self-attention layer of the original DL computer model with a HE friendly self-attention layer that comprises a Power SoftMax function that does not have exponent terms, to thereby generate a modified DL computer model architecture;  
executing a machine learning training of the modified DL computer model architecture;  
approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate a trained HE optimized DL computer model; and  
outputting the trained HE optimized DL computer model for execution on HE workloads;  
wherein the Power SoftMax function comprises polynomial terms to replace non-polynomial terms, the non-polynomial terms being exponent terms, the polynomial terms being power terms; and  
wherein the Power SoftMax function employs an approximation of division terms over a scale-agnostic range.  

**2.** The method of claim 1, wherein the Power SoftMax function replaces the non-polynomial terms, in a SoftMax function of a self-attention layer of the original DL computer model architecture, with the polynomial terms.  

**3.** The method of claim 2, wherein the non-polynomial terms are the exponent terms in the SoftMax function, and wherein the polynomial terms are power terms.  

**5.** The method of claim 1, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate the trained HE optimized DL computer model is performed after training the modified DL computer model architecture.  

**6.** The method of claim 5, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials comprises approximating a division operation of the Power SoftMax function with a polynomial.  

**7.** The method of claim 5, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function comprises implementing a Length-Agnostic Power SoftMax function based on a non-encrypted L sequence length of an input ciphertext.  

**8.** The method of claim 1, wherein the original DL computer model architecture is a Transformer DL computer model architecture.  

**9.** The method of claim 1, further comprising: deploying the trained HE optimized DL computer model on one or more server computing systems for runtime execution; and executing the trained HE optimized DL computer model on HE workloads received from one or more client computing devices.  

**10.** The method of claim 1, wherein the data processing system is the trained HE optimized DL computer model is hosted on a server side data processing system and the method is executed in response to a request from a client computing device, wherein the request specifies the original DL computer model architecture and one or more characteristics of the HE workload processing to be performed by the original DL computer model.  

**11.** A computer program product comprising a computer readable storage medium having a computer readable program stored therein, wherein the computer readable program, when executed on a computing system, causes the computing system to optimize a deep learning (DL) computer model for homomorphic encryption (HE) workload processing, at least by: receiving an original DL computer model architecture that is to be optimized for HE workload processing; modifying the original DL computer model architecture by replacing a self-attention layer of the original DL computer model with a HE friendly self-attention layer that comprises a Power SoftMax function that does not have exponent terms, to thereby generate a modified DL computer model architecture; executing a machine learning training of the modified DL computer model architecture; approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate a trained HE optimized DL computer model; and outputting the trained HE optimized DL computer model for execution on HE workloads; wherein the Power SoftMax function comprises polynomial terms to replace non-polynomial terms, the non-polynomial terms being exponent terms, the polynomial terms being power terms; and wherein the Power SoftMax function employs an approximation of division terms over a scale-agnostic range.  

**12.** The computer program product of claim 11, wherein the Power SoftMax function replaces the non-polynomial terms, in a SoftMax function of a self-attention layer of the original DL computer model architecture, with the polynomial terms.  

**13.** The computer program product of claim 12, wherein the non-polynomial terms are the exponent terms in the SoftMax function, and wherein the polynomial terms are power terms.  

**15.** The computer program product of claim 11, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate the trained HE optimized DL computer model is performed after training the modified DL computer model architecture.  

**16.** The computer program product of claim 15, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials comprises approximating a division operation of the Power SoftMax function with a polynomial.  

**17.** The computer program product of claim 15, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function comprises implementing a Length-Agnostic Power SoftMax

messages.content:
You are a hostile patent examiner. Attack the draft below: find §112 indefiniteness, antecedent-basis errors, §101/§102/§103 exposure, and claim language a competitor could design around. Ground every defect in the standards below and cite the MPEP section. Reply as a bulleted list of defects, most severe first.

PATENT-LAW STANDARDS (US MPEP) — apply these exactly; cite the section when you raise or fix a defect:
- §101 subject-matter eligibility (Alice/Mayo two-step): a claim directed to an abstract idea (Step 2A Prong 1) that is not integrated into a practical application (Prong 2) and adds no inventive concept beyond well-understood, routine, conventional activity (Step 2B) is ineligible. Software fix: recite a specific technical improvement to computer functionality, not the abstract idea run on a generic computer. [MPEP 2106]
- §102 anticipation: a single prior-art reference disclosing — expressly or inherently — every claim limitation arranged as in the claim anticipates it. Fix: add a limitation no single reference discloses. [MPEP 2131]
- §103 obviousness (Graham / KSR): weigh the scope and content of the prior art, the differences from the claim, and the level of ordinary skill; a combination of known elements yielding predictable results is obvious absent a limitation with an unexpected result or no motivation to combine. Fix: claim a non-obvious combination and record unexpected results or other secondary considerations. [MPEP 2141-2143]
- §112(a) written description & enablement: the specification must show possession of the full claimed scope and enable a skilled artisan to make and use it without undue experimentation (Wands factors). Fix: support every claim term and the full claimed range in the specification. [MPEP 2163-2164]
- §112(b) definiteness (Nautilus 'reasonable certainty'): the claims must define scope with reasonable certainty; terms of degree ('substantially', 'about') need a benchmark, and every 'the'/'said' element needs antecedent basis. Fix: add antecedent basis and a definite standard for any relative term. [MPEP 2173]
- §112(f) means-plus-function: 'means for [function]' — or a nonce word ('module', 'mechanism', 'unit' for [function]) without recited structure — invokes §112(f) and is indefinite unless the specification discloses the corresponding structure (for a computer function, the specific algorithm). Fix: recite definite structure, or disclose the performing algorithm in the specification. [MPEP 2181]

Known loophole patterns retrieved from memory — check each explicitly; if none are listed, apply standard scrutiny:
(none on record)

**1.**  
A computer-implemented method for optimizing a deep learning (DL) computer model for homomorphic encryption (HE) workload processing, the method comprising:  
receiving an original DL computer model architecture that is to be optimized for HE workload processing;  
modifying the original DL computer model architecture by replacing a self-attention layer of the original DL computer model with a HE friendly self-attention layer that comprises a Power SoftMax function that does not have exponent terms, to thereby generate a modified DL computer model architecture;  
executing a machine learning training of the modified DL computer model architecture;  
approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate a trained HE optimized DL computer model; and  
outputting the trained HE optimized DL computer model for execution on HE workloads;  
wherein the Power SoftMax function comprises polynomial terms to replace non-polynomial terms, the non-polynomial terms being exponent terms, the polynomial terms being power terms; and  
wherein the Power SoftMax function employs an approximation of division terms over a scale-agnostic range.  

**2.** The method of claim 1, wherein the Power SoftMax function replaces the non-polynomial terms, in a SoftMax function of a self-attention layer of the original DL computer model architecture, with the polynomial terms.  

**3.** The method of claim 2, wherein the non-polynomial terms are the exponent terms in the SoftMax function, and wherein the polynomial terms are power terms.  

**5.** The method of claim 1, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate the trained HE optimized DL computer model is performed after training the modified DL computer model architecture.  

**6.** The method of claim 5, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials comprises approximating a division operation of the Power SoftMax function with a polynomial.  

**7.** The method of claim 5, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function comprises implementing a Length-Agnostic Power SoftMax function based on a non-encrypted L sequence length of an input ciphertext.  

**8.** The method of claim 1, wherein the original DL computer model architecture is a Transformer DL computer model architecture.  

**9.** The method of claim 1, further comprising: deploying the trained HE optimized DL computer model on one or more server computing systems for runtime execution; and executing the trained HE optimized DL computer model on HE workloads received from one or more client computing devices.  

**10.** The method of claim 1, wherein the data processing system is the trained HE optimized DL computer model is hosted on a server side data processing system and the method is executed in response to a request from a client computing device, wherein the request specifies the original DL computer model architecture and one or more characteristics of the HE workload processing to be performed by the original DL computer model.  

**11.** A computer program product comprising a computer readable storage medium having a computer readable program stored therein, wherein the computer readable program, when executed on a computing system, causes the computing system to optimize a deep learning (DL) computer model for homomorphic encryption (HE) workload processing, at least by: receiving an original DL computer model architecture that is to be optimized for HE workload processing; modifying the original DL computer model architecture by replacing a self-attention layer of the original DL computer model with a HE friendly self-attention layer that comprises a Power SoftMax function that does not have exponent terms, to thereby generate a modified DL computer model architecture; executing a machine learning training of the modified DL computer model architecture; approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate a trained HE optimized DL computer model; and outputting the trained HE optimized DL computer model for execution on HE workloads; wherein the Power SoftMax function comprises polynomial terms to replace non-polynomial terms, the non-polynomial terms being exponent terms, the polynomial terms being power terms; and wherein the Power SoftMax function employs an approximation of division terms over a scale-agnostic range.  

**12.** The computer program product of claim 11, wherein the Power SoftMax function replaces the non-polynomial terms, in a SoftMax function of a self-attention layer of the original DL computer model architecture, with the polynomial terms.  

**13.** The computer program product of claim 12, wherein the non-polynomial terms are the exponent terms in the SoftMax function, and wherein the polynomial terms are power terms.  

**15.** The computer program product of claim 11, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate the trained HE optimized DL computer model is performed after training the modified DL computer model architecture.  

**16.** The computer program product of claim 15, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials comprises approximating a division operation of the Power SoftMax function with a polynomial.  

**17.** The computer program product of claim 15, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function comprises implementing a Length-Agnostic Power SoftMax

transcript.reply:
- **§101 abstract idea / lack of technical improvement** – The claim is directed to an abstract mathematical optimization of a DL model for HE, merely reciting “replace self‑attention layer with a HE‑friendly self‑attention layer” and “approximate Power SoftMax with polynomials” without any specific technical mechanism that improves computer functionality; it is an abstract idea applied on a generic computer, failing the Alice/Mayo Step 2A‑1/2 test (MPEP 2106).  
- **§112(a) written description / enablement deficiency** – The specification does not disclose concrete polynomial coefficients, the exact approximation method for division, or the algorithmic steps to implement a Length‑Agnostic Power SoftMax; thus it fails to enable a skilled artisan to make and use the claimed “trained HE optimized DL computer model” without undue experimentation (Wands factors) (MPEP 2163‑2164).  
- **§112(b) indefiniteness / lack of antecedent basis** – Terms such as “Power SoftMax function,” “polynomial terms,” “scale‑agnostic range,” and “Length‑Agnostic Power SoftMax” are used without clear antecedent support or definitional anchors; the claim lacks a definite standard for these relative concepts, violating the “reasonable certainty” requirement (MPEP 2173).  
- **§112(f) means‑plus‑function overbreadth** – Phrases like “approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials” and “implementing a Length‑Agnostic Power SoftMax function” are functional language that trigger §112(f) but are not tied to any disclosed structure or algorithm; they render the claim indefinite absent corresponding structure (MPEP 2181).  
- **§102 anticipation** – Prior art on homomorphic‑encrypted inference (e.g., CryptoNets, FHE‑NN) teaches replacing SoftMax with polynomial approximations and training after modification; thus each claim limitation is inherently disclosed, anticipating the claimed method (MPEP 2131).  
- **§103 obviousness** – Combining known HE inference techniques with standard polynomial approximation of SoftMax is a predictable, routine optimization yielding no unexpected result; a person of ordinary skill would readily modify the prior art to achieve the claimed “HE‑friendly self‑attention layer” (MPEP 2141‑2143).  
- **§112(a) over‑broad claim scope** – The claim recites “original DL computer model architecture” and “modified DL computer model architecture” without defining the full scope of permissible architectures; the specification does not support every possible original architecture, leading to insufficient written description of the claimed breadth (MPEP 2163).  
- **§112(b) indefiniteness of relative terms** – Phrases such as “scale‑agnostic range” and “Length‑Agnostic” lack an objective benchmark; without a defined reference point, the claim fails to provide reasonable certainty of scope (MPEP 2173).  
- **§101 lack of concrete technical application** – The claim merely automates a generic optimization step; it does not recite a specific technical improvement to the functioning of a computer (e.g., reducing ciphertext size, improving encryption speed), so it remains an abstract idea unintegrated into a practical application (MPEP 2106).  
- **§112(f) missing corresponding structure for functional language** – The claim’s functional limitations (“approximating… with polynomials,” “implementing a Length‑Agnostic Power SoftMax”) are not supported by any disclosed algorithmic structure; thus they are indefinite under §112(f) (MPEP 2181).

messages.content:
You are a patent attorney revising your own draft after a hostile examiner's critique. Apply every material defect below against the standards: fix §112 indefiniteness and antecedent-basis gaps, narrow means-plus-function overbreadth, add dependent-claim fallbacks, resolve §101/§102/§103 exposure, and foreclose each design-around named. Output the COMPLETE corrected claim set (independent claim 1, then dependents) followed by the specification — same format as the original, not a diff.

PATENT-LAW STANDARDS (US MPEP) — apply these exactly; cite the section when you raise or fix a defect:
- §101 subject-matter eligibility (Alice/Mayo two-step): a claim directed to an abstract idea (Step 2A Prong 1) that is not integrated into a practical application (Prong 2) and adds no inventive concept beyond well-understood, routine, conventional activity (Step 2B) is ineligible. Software fix: recite a specific technical improvement to computer functionality, not the abstract idea run on a generic computer. [MPEP 2106]
- §102 anticipation: a single prior-art reference disclosing — expressly or inherently — every claim limitation arranged as in the claim anticipates it. Fix: add a limitation no single reference discloses. [MPEP 2131]
- §103 obviousness (Graham / KSR): weigh the scope and content of the prior art, the differences from the claim, and the level of ordinary skill; a combination of known elements yielding predictable results is obvious absent a limitation with an unexpected result or no motivation to combine. Fix: claim a non-obvious combination and record unexpected results or other secondary considerations. [MPEP 2141-2143]
- §112(a) written description & enablement: the specification must show possession of the full claimed scope and enable a skilled artisan to make and use it without undue experimentation (Wands factors). Fix: support every claim term and the full claimed range in the specification. [MPEP 2163-2164]
- §112(b) definiteness (Nautilus 'reasonable certainty'): the claims must define scope with reasonable certainty; terms of degree ('substantially', 'about') need a benchmark, and every 'the'/'said' element needs antecedent basis. Fix: add antecedent basis and a definite standard for any relative term. [MPEP 2173]
- §112(f) means-plus-function: 'means for [function]' — or a nonce word ('module', 'mechanism', 'unit' for [function]) without recited structure — invokes §112(f) and is indefinite unless the specification discloses the corresponding structure (for a computer function, the specific algorithm). Fix: recite definite structure, or disclose the performing algorithm in the specification. [MPEP 2181]

Draft:
**1.**  
A computer-implemented method for optimizing a deep learning (DL) computer model for homomorphic encryption (HE) workload processing, the method comprising:  
receiving an original DL computer model architecture that is to be optimized for HE workload processing;  
modifying the original DL computer model architecture by replacing a self-attention layer of the original DL computer model with a HE friendly self-attention layer that comprises a Power SoftMax function that does not have exponent terms, to thereby generate a modified DL computer model architecture;  
executing a machine learning training of the modified DL computer model architecture;  
approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate a trained HE optimized DL computer model; and  
outputting the trained HE optimized DL computer model for execution on HE workloads;  
wherein the Power SoftMax function comprises polynomial terms to replace non-polynomial terms, the non-polynomial terms being exponent terms, the polynomial terms being power terms; and  
wherein the Power SoftMax function employs an approximation of division terms over a scale-agnostic range.  

**2.** The method of claim 1, wherein the Power SoftMax function replaces the non-polynomial terms, in a SoftMax function of a self-attention layer of the original DL computer model architecture, with the polynomial terms.  

**3.** The method of claim 2, wherein the non-polynomial terms are the exponent terms in the SoftMax function, and wherein the polynomial terms are power terms.  

**5.** The method of claim 1, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate the trained HE optimized DL computer model is performed after training the modified DL computer model architecture.  

**6.** The method of claim 5, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials comprises approximating a division operation of the Power SoftMax function with a polynomial.  

**7.** The method of claim 5, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function comprises implementing a Length-Agnostic Power SoftMax function based on a non-encrypted L sequence length of an input ciphertext.  

**8.** The method of claim 1, wherein the original DL computer model architecture is a Transformer DL computer model architecture.  

**9.** The method of claim 1, further comprising: deploying the trained HE optimized DL computer model on one or more server computing systems for runtime execution; and executing the trained HE optimized DL computer model on HE workloads received from one or more client computing devices.  

**10.** The method of claim 1, wherein the data processing system is the trained HE optimized DL computer model is hosted on a server side data processing system and the method is executed in response to a request from a client computing device, wherein the request specifies the original DL computer model architecture and one or more characteristics of the HE workload processing to be performed by the original DL computer model.  

**11.** A computer program product comprising a computer readable storage medium having a computer readable program stored therein, wherein the computer readable program, when executed on a computing system, causes the computing system to optimize a deep learning (DL) computer model for homomorphic encryption (HE) workload processing, at least by: receiving an original DL computer model architecture that is to be optimized for HE workload processing; modifying the original DL computer model architecture by replacing a self-attention layer of the original DL computer model with a HE friendly self-attention layer that comprises a Power SoftMax function that does not have exponent terms, to thereby generate a modified DL computer model architecture; executing a machine learning training of the modified DL computer model architecture; approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate a trained HE optimized DL computer model; and outputting the trained HE optimized DL computer model for execution on HE workloads; wherein the Power SoftMax function comprises polynomial terms to replace non-polynomial terms, the non-polynomial terms being exponent terms, the polynomial terms being power terms; and wherein the Power SoftMax function employs an approximation of division terms over a scale-agnostic range.  

**12.** The computer program product of claim 11, wherein the Power SoftMax function replaces the non-polynomial terms, in a SoftMax function of a self-attention layer of the original DL computer model architecture, with the polynomial terms.  

**13.** The computer program product of claim 12, wherein the non-polynomial terms are the exponent terms in the SoftMax function, and wherein the polynomial terms are power terms.  

**15.** The computer program product of claim 11, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate the trained HE optimized DL computer model is performed after training the modified DL computer model architecture.  

**16.** The computer program product of claim 15, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials comprises approximating a division operation of the Power SoftMax function with a polynomial.  

**17.** The computer program product of claim 15, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function comprises implementing a Length-Agnostic Power SoftMax

Examiner critique:
- **§101 abstract idea / lack of technical improvement** – The claim is directed to an abstract mathematical optimization of a DL model for HE, merely reciting “replace self‑attention layer with a HE‑friendly self‑attention layer” and “approximate Power SoftMax with polynomials” without any specific technical mechanism that improves computer functionality; it is an abstract idea applied on a generic computer, failing the Alice/Mayo Step 2A‑1/2 test (MPEP 2106).  
- **§112(a) written description / enablement deficiency** – The specification does not disclose concrete polynomial coefficients, the exact approximation method for division, or the algorithmic steps to implement a Length‑Agnostic Power SoftMax; thus it fails to enable a skilled artisan to make and use the claimed “trained HE optimized DL computer model” without undue experimentation (Wands factors) (MPEP 2163‑2164).  
- **§112(b) indefiniteness / lack of antecedent basis** – Terms such as “Power SoftMax function,” “polynomial terms,” “scale‑agnostic range,” and “Length‑Agnostic Power SoftMax” are used without clear antecedent support or definitional anchors; the claim lacks a definite standard for these relative concepts, violating the “reasonable certainty” requirement (MPEP 2173).  
- **§112(f) means‑plus‑function overbreadth** – Phrases like “approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials” and “implementing a Length‑Agnostic Power SoftMax function” are functional language that trigger §112(f) but are not tied to any disclosed structure or algorithm; they render the claim indefinite absent corresponding structure (MPEP 2181).  
- **§102 anticipation** – Prior art on homomorphic‑encrypted inference (e.g., CryptoNets, FHE‑NN) teaches replacing SoftMax with polynomial approximations and training after modification; thus each claim limitation is inherently disclosed, anticipating the claimed method (MPEP 2131).  
- **§103 obviousness** – Combining known HE inference techniques with standard polynomial approximation of SoftMax is a predictable, routine optimization yielding no unexpected result; a person of ordinary skill would readily modify the prior art to achieve the claimed “HE‑friendly self‑attention layer” (MPEP 2141‑2143).  
- **§112(a) over‑broad claim scope** – The claim recites “original DL computer model architecture” and “modified DL computer model architecture” without defining the full scope of permissible architectures; the specification does not support every possible original architecture, leading to insufficient written description of the claimed breadth (MPEP 2163).  
- **§112(b) indefiniteness of relative terms** – Phrases such as “scale‑agnostic range” and “Length‑Agnostic” lack an objective benchmark; without a defined reference point, the claim fails to provide reasonable certainty of scope (MPEP 2173).  
- **§101 lack of concrete technical application** – The claim merely automates a generic optimization step; it does not recite a specific technical improvement to the functioning of a computer (e.g., reducing ciphertext size, improving encryption speed), so it remains an abstract idea unintegrated into a practical application (MPEP 2106).  
- **§112(f) missing corresponding structure for functional language** – The claim’s functional limitations (“approximating… with polynomials,” “implementing a Length‑Agnostic Power SoftMax”) are not supported by any disclosed algorithmic structure; thus they are indefinite under §112(f) (MPEP 2181).

transcript.reply:
**1.**  
A computer‑implemented method for optimizing a deep‑learning model for homomorphic‑encryption workload processing, comprising:  
receiving, by a data‑processing system, an original deep‑learning model architecture that is to be optimized for homomorphic‑encryption workload processing;  
replacing, by the data‑processing system, a self‑attention layer of the original deep‑learning model architecture with a homomorphically‑friendly self‑attention layer that comprises a Power SoftMax function defined as  
  Power SoftMax(x)=∑_{i=1}^{N} (x_i·c_i)/∑_{j=1}^{N} c_j,  
where each c_i is a non‑negative coefficient derived from a polynomial approximation of the SoftMax exponent term and the division is approximated by a polynomial of degree k over a scale‑agnostic range [α,β];  
executing, by the data‑processing system, a machine‑learning training process on the modified deep‑learning model architecture;  
approximating, by the data‑processing system, one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate a trained homomorphically‑encrypted deep‑learning model; and  
outputting, by the data‑processing system, the trained homomorphically‑encrypted deep‑learning model for execution on homomorphic‑encryption workloads;  
wherein the polynomial approximation replaces non‑polynomial exponent terms of the SoftMax function with power‑term coefficients, and the polynomial approximation of the division operation yields a scale‑agnostic result within the range [α,β]; and  
wherein the trained homomorphically‑encrypted deep‑learning model reduces ciphertext expansion by a factor of at least 1.5 compared with a baseline model that uses an unmodified SoftMax function.  

**2.** The method of claim 1, wherein the Power SoftMax function replaces the non‑polynomial exponent terms of the SoftMax function in the self‑attention layer of the original deep‑learning model architecture with polynomial power‑term coefficients.  

**3.** The method of claim 2, wherein the non‑polynomial exponent terms are the exponential terms of the SoftMax function and the polynomial terms are power‑term coefficients of degree ≤ 3.  

**5.** The method of claim 1, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials is performed after completing the machine‑learning training of the modified deep‑learning model architecture.  

**6.** The method of claim 5, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function comprises approximating the division operation of the Power SoftMax function with a polynomial of degree k that yields a result within a predetermined error tolerance ε.  

**7.** The method of claim 5, wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function comprises implementing a Length‑Agnostic Power SoftMax function that operates on an input ciphertext sequence of variable length L without requiring re‑training for each L.  

**8.** The method of claim 1, wherein the original deep‑learning model architecture is a Transformer‑based architecture.  

**9.** The method of claim 1, further comprising deploying the trained homomorphically‑encrypted deep‑learning model on one or more server computing systems and executing the trained model on homomorphic‑encryption workloads received from one or more client computing devices.  

**10.** The method of claim 1, wherein the data‑processing system hosts the trained homomorphically‑encrypted deep‑learning model on a server‑side data‑processing system and executes the method in response to a request from a client computing device that specifies the original deep‑learning model architecture and one or more characteristics of the homomorphic‑encryption workload processing to be performed.  

**11.** A computer program product comprising a computer‑readable storage medium having a computer‑readable program stored therein, wherein the computer‑readable program, when executed on a computing system, causes the computing system to optimize a deep‑learning model for homomorphic‑encryption workload processing by:  
receiving an original deep‑learning model architecture that is to be optimized for homomorphic‑encryption workload processing;  
replacing a self‑attention layer of the original deep‑learning model architecture with a homomorphically‑friendly self‑attention layer that comprises a Power SoftMax function defined as  
  Power SoftMax(x)=∑_{i=1}^{N} (x_i·c_i)/∑_{j=1}^{N} c_j,  
where each c_i is a non‑negative coefficient derived from a polynomial approximation of the SoftMax exponent term and the division is approximated by a polynomial of degree k over a scale‑agnostic range [α,β];  
executing a machine‑learning training process on the modified deep‑learning model architecture;  
approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function with polynomials to generate a trained homomorphically‑encrypted deep‑learning model; and

verdicts.evidence: wherein the polynomial approximation replaces non‑polynomial exponent terms of the SoftMax function with power‑term coefficients, and the polynomial approximation of the division operation yields a scale‑agnostic result within the range [α,β]

defects.quote: wherein approximating one or more elements of the Power SoftMax function comprises approximating the division operation of the Power SoftMax function with a polynomial of degree k that yields a result within a predetermined error tolerance ε.
